case-converter.title: Text Case Converter
case-converter.description: Convert text between camelCase, snake_case, PascalCase, and more.
case-converter.article: Easily switch between different naming conventions used in programming.

faqs.a0: Yes, kebab-case, snake_case, camelCase, and PascalCase are all included.

json-to-csv.title: JSON to CSV Converter
json-to-csv.description: Convert JSON data to CSV format with header detection.
json-to-csv.placeholder: Paste JSON array or object here...
json-to-csv.article: Convert structured JSON data into flat CSV format. Ideal for spreadsheet imports and data analysis.

faqs.a0: Yes, nested objects are flattened using dot notation.

json-formatter.title: JSON Formatter
json-formatter.description: Clean and format JSON data with ease.
json-formatter.article: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data-interchange format. Our JSON Formatter helps you beautify minified JSON, sort keys alphabetically, and even escape unicode characters for safe transmission.

faqs.q0: Is my JSON data sent to a server?
faqs.a0: No, all formatting is done entirely in your browser. Your data is 100% secure and private.
faqs.a1: Yes! Use the Minify toggle in the customization settings to remove all whitespace.

csv-to-json.title: CSV to JSON Converter
csv-to-json.description: Convert CSV data to JSON format with multi-row header support.
csv-to-json.article: Transform comma-separated values into JSON. Supports custom delimiters and header detection.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can specify the delimiter in the settings.

xml-formatter.title: XML Formatter
xml-formatter.description: Beautify and format XML data with syntax highlighting.
xml-formatter.article: Clean up messy XML code with proper indentation and node collapsing.

faqs.a0: Yes, it will highlight syntax errors if the XML is malformed.

yaml-validator.title: YAML Validator
yaml-validator.description: Validate and lint YAML data with line-level errors.
yaml-validator.article: Check your YAML files for syntax errors and structural validity. Supports YAML 1.2 standards.

toml-validator.title: TOML Validator
toml-validator.description: Check TOML syntax and preview as JSON.
toml-validator.article: Validate TOML configuration files and see how they map to JSON objects.

markdown-previewer.title: Markdown Previewer
markdown-previewer.description: Live preview GitHub-flavored Markdown with styling.
markdown-previewer.article: Write Markdown and see the rendered HTML instantly. Supports tables, task lists, and syntax highlighting.

faqs.a0: Yes, all standard GFM features like tables and task lists work perfectly.

word-counter.title: Word & Char Counter
word-counter.description: Count words, characters, lines, and reading time.
word-counter.article: Get detailed metrics for your writing, including readability scores and character counts with/without spaces.

faqs.a0: It's based on an average reading speed of 200-250 words per minute.

lorem-ipsum.title: Lorem Ipsum Generator
lorem-ipsum.description: Generate custom placeholder text for your designs.
lorem-ipsum.article: Generate standard Lorem Ipsum text by paragraphs, sentences, or words.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can specify the exact byte count for the output.

slug-generator.title: Slug Generator
slug-generator.description: Generate URL-safe slugs by stripping special characters.
slug-generator.article: Create SEO-friendly URLs by converting titles into lowercase, hyphenated strings.

faqs.a0: Yes, all non-alphanumeric characters except hyphens are stripped.

duplicate-remover.title: Duplicate Line Remover
duplicate-remover.description: Remove duplicate lines, sort, and trim whitespace.
duplicate-remover.article: Clean up lists by removing repeated entries and normalizing whitespace.

faqs.a0: You can toggle case-sensitivity in the settings.

whitespace-remover.title: Whitespace Remover
whitespace-remover.description: Trim all, leading, trailing, or extra whitespace.
whitespace-remover.article: Remove unnecessary spaces from your text to make it cleaner and more compact.

faqs.a0: Yes, the 'Strip All' option removes every single space character.

ascii-art.title: Text to ASCII Art
ascii-art.description: Convert text into stylized ASCII banner fonts.
ascii-art.article: Turn your text into large ASCII art banners for use in code comments or terminal headers.

faqs.a0: Yes, multiple classic ASCII fonts like 'Big', 'Slant', and 'Shadow' are available.

emoji-picker.title: Emoji Picker & Search
emoji-picker.description: Search and copy emojis for your projects.
emoji-picker.article: Find the perfect emoji quickly and copy it with a single click. Includes all the latest Emoji 15.0 additions.

faqs.a0: Yes, they are standard unicode emojis that work across all modern platforms.

text-sorter.title: Text Sorter
text-sorter.description: Sort text lines alphabetically, numerically, or shuffle them.
text-sorter.article: Organize lists of data quickly with various sorting algorithms.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can sort in both ascending and descending order.

line-number-adder.title: Line Number Adder
line-number-adder.description: Add custom line numbers, padding, and delimiters to text.
line-number-adder.article: Enumerate lines of text or code with customizable formatting.

faqs.q0: Can I start from a specific number?
faqs.a0: Yes, the starting number and increment are both configurable.

find-replace.title: Find & Replace Tool
find-replace.description: Advanced find and replace with regex and case support.
find-replace.article: Perform bulk text transformations using simple strings or powerful regular expressions.

faqs.a0: Yes, full JavaScript regular expression support is included.

string-reverse.title: String Reverse
string-reverse.description: Reverse characters or words with instant output.
string-reverse.article: Quickly flip your text backwards or reverse the order of words.

faqs.a0: Yes, there is a toggle to reverse words instead of individual characters.

code-diff-checker.title: Code Diff Checker
code-diff-checker.description: Compare two blocks of code to find differences.
code-diff-checker.article: A Code Diff Checker compares two text files or code snippets to highlight the differences. It is an essential tool for reviewing changes, debugging, and understanding modifications between code versions.

faqs.q0: What is the difference between Split and Unified views?
faqs.a0: Split view shows the old and new code side-by-side. Unified view shows all changes linearly in a single column.
faqs.a1: Yes, simply toggle 'Ignore Whitespace' to focus only on structural code changes.

morse-converter.title: Morse Code Converter
morse-converter.description: Translate text to Morse code and vice versa.
morse-converter.article: Morse code is a method of encoding text characters as sequences of dots (·) and dashes (−), developed by Samuel Morse in the 1830s. Each letter, number, and punctuation mark has a unique pattern. Morse code was historically used for long-distance communication via telegraph and radio, and remains relevant today in aviation, amateur radio, and assistive technology for people with disabilities.

faqs.q0: How do I read Morse code?
faqs.a0: Dots (·) represent short signals and dashes (−) represent long signals. Letters are separated by spaces, and words by longer spaces. For example, SOS is ··· −−− ··· (three dots, three dashes, three dots).
faqs.q1: Can I hear the Morse code?
faqs.a1: Yes! This tool includes an audio playback feature that converts the Morse code into audible beeps, with short beeps for dots and longer beeps for dashes, just like traditional telegraph communication.

morse-converter.info: Convert plain text into Morse code dots and dashes, or translate Morse back into text. You can also play the Morse code as audio.

unicode-inspector.title: Unicode Inspector
unicode-inspector.description: Inspect Unicode characters, code points, and categories.
unicode-inspector.article: Unicode is a universal character encoding standard that assigns a unique number (code point) to every character across all writing systems, symbols, and emoji. Code points are typically written in hexadecimal with a U+ prefix (e.g., U+0041 for 'A'). This inspector helps developers debug text encoding issues, understand character composition, and identify invisible or special characters that may cause problems in code or data.

faqs.a0: A code point is Unicode's unique identifier for each character, written as U+ followed by hexadecimal digits. For example, U+0041 is 'A', U+1F600 is 😀. Code points range from U+0000 to U+10FFFF.
faqs.q1: Why do some characters show multiple code points?
faqs.a1: Some characters are composed of multiple code points, like emoji with skin tone modifiers or accented letters using combining diacritical marks. For example, 👨‍👩‍👧 (family) is actually multiple code points joined together.

unicode-inspector.info: Inspect string characters to reveal their exact Unicode hex code points and decimal representations.
